canonical_question: When should SaaS use seat-based vs usage-based vs hybrid pricing - constraints and revenue impact?
constraints: Benchmarks skew toward VC-backed B2B SaaS; bootstrapped or SMB-focused companies may see different NRR and growth patterns
AI features run 50-60% gross margins vs 80-90% for traditional SaaS, making seat-based pricing infeasible for compute-heavy features
Usage-based requires metering infrastructure and real-time billing systems before adoption
78% of IT leaders experienced unexpected charges; hybrid model complexity increases billing disputes and extends sales cycles
Enterprise buyers in regulated industries may require fixed annual contracts, limiting usage-based components

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs AI-specific pricing guidance (tokens, credits, outcome-based)
User needs general SaaS financial benchmarks (CAC, LTV, Rule of 40)
User is comparing specific SaaS vendors rather than pricing strategy

inputs_needed.question: What type of SaaS product are you pricing?
options: Collaboration/productivity tool (value scales with team size)
Multi-product platform (different value drivers per module)

inputs_needed.question: What is the company's current growth stage?
options: Pre-revenue / MVP (need to attract first customers)
Scale-up (need revenue expansion from existing customers)
Mature (need to defend NRR and reduce churn)
